claim: Claude's performance on complex reasoning tasks scales with the amount of time given for thinking - providing more tokens for thinking budget improves results on harder problems
actionable: Use Claude's extended thinking API feature and allocate higher token budgets for complex reasoning tasks, lower budgets for simple queries
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Context window management is critical for agent performance - combining memory tools with context editing improved internal benchmarks by 39%
actionable: Implement memory storage for patterns and preferences external to the context window, use context editing to remove irrelevant tool results and past context
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) enables standardized agent-external-system interaction and provides better performance than agents limited to prompted context alone
actionable: Adopt MCP for integrating external systems like GitHub and Sentry into agent workflows, providing access to real-time context and tools
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Claude reliably uses tools when given proper definitions - requires name, description, and input schema - and can manage hundreds of tool calls in a single session
actionable: Define custom tools with clear names, descriptions, and JSON input schemas; Claude will learn to call them appropriately without explicit prompting for each invocation
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Giving Claude access to code execution in a sandboxed environment allows for autonomous execution of tasks - code execution tool removes need for user to manually run generated code
actionable: Use Claude's code execution API to allow direct code execution in secure sandboxes; this enables autonomous task completion without user intervention
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Agent skills (folders of scripts, instructions, resources) combined with MCP tools enable domain-specific expertise - Claude learns when to invoke skills based on task context
actionable: Create skill folders containing relevant scripts and patterns for your domain; Claude will automatically select and apply appropriate skills during execution
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Sub-agent orchestration with separation of concerns reduces context pollution and improves agent efficiency - injection of minimal necessary context per sub-agent reduces compression needs from 35 to 4-5 recompressions
actionable: Delegate specific subtasks to sub-agents with fresh context; inject only necessary context and return results to main loop, reducing recompression overhead
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Sub-agent testing workflows are effective for verification - move browser observation and testing into separate sub-agents to avoid confusing main loop with heterogeneous actions
actionable: Create dedicated sub-agents for testing and verification; return only final test results to main agent loop to reduce context noise
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Embedding tools into developer workflows (via IDEs/MCP integration) is key to adoption - standalone tools without IDE integration saw low engagement despite good feature quality
actionable: Integrate AI tools directly into developer IDEs and workflows rather than creating separate interfaces; embedding drives adoption more than feature quality alone
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Long-running asynchronous agents should not require developer babysitting - modern models can execute end-to-end autonomously when context is clear
actionable: Design async agents to run autonomously without requiring developer monitoring; provide clear goal context upfront and let agents handle execution end-to-end
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Context switching costs developers up to 40% of productive time - minimizing manual task management and monitoring improves developer velocity significantly
actionable: Use agents to handle task execution and monitoring; minimize manual context switching by automating routine checks and status updates
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Gathering context for debugging (API docs, logs, external resources) is a major pain point in app maintenance - automating context collection significantly speeds up fix cycles
actionable: Use diagnostic agents to automatically gather logs, API documentation, and related context; embed this into support workflows to reduce triage time
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: AI code generation tools require strong integration into existing IDEs and processes to drive adoption - standalone playgrounds see poor engagement
actionable: Integrate codegen tools into IDEs your users already use; avoid requiring context switches to separate web interfaces or playgrounds
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Larger context windows (1M+ tokens) combined with proper context editing tools maximize agent performance - managing what's in the window is as important as window size
actionable: Take advantage of large context windows but actively remove irrelevant context; balance window size with active curation of current context
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex

claim: Support teams can be empowered to ship code fixes through structured guardrails and AI-assisted tooling - starting with narrow scope (4 target apps) and engineering review gates
actionable: Pilot code-shipping permissions with limited scope; require engineering review for initial deployments; gradually expand scope as confidence and process mature
source_title: AIE CODE 2025: AI Leadership ft Anthropic, OpenAI, McKinsey, Bloomberg, Google Deepmind, and Tenex
